chunks.title: Cortisol to DHEA Ratio and Longevity
chunks.summary: The speaker discusses the importance of the cortisol to DHEA (DHEAS) ratio in relation to health and longevity. They emphasize that a lower cortisol level and a higher DHEA level are desirable for reducing the risk of mortality. They reference a study that supports this ratio as a significant indicator of longevity.

chunks.title: Dietary Influences on Health
chunks.summary: The speaker highlights the necessity of incorporating carbohydrates and nutrients from meat and organs into one's diet to maintain a favorable cortisol to DHEA ratio. They argue against common practices in the longevity space such as caloric restriction and fasting, asserting these can elevate cortisol levels, which is detrimental to longevity.
chunks.transcript: that's a pretty significant that's like three times higher risk with an increased higher cortisol to dheas ratio you want to know what your cortisol to dheas ratio is and you want to keep it as low as possible which means giving your body signals of abundance things like meat things like carbohydrates things like sleep things like sunlight giving your body food throughout the day not fasting not a fan of fasting I've spoken about that in the past and not restricting calories obviously some people will say how am I going to lose weight if I don't restrict calories and I will say this the important thing to consider here is that if you want to lose weight applying moderation and discipline is going to be necessary but I think applying moderation and discipline to the amount of food you eat or when you eat it is the wrong use of your efforts apply that moderation and discipline to the quality of the food you eat I think that there is no better way to long-term health and sustainable weight loss than to improve your food quality what is improved food quality Paul it's that your great great great grandmother would recognize as unprocessed meat and plant Foods as you can eat that is high quality food the more high quality food you eat the more full you will be the easier it will be to lose weight that is the way to sustainable weight loss counting calories over fasting over stressing your body again elevates all the stress

chunks.title: Moderation and Food Quality
chunks.summary: The speaker advocates for focusing on the quality of food rather than calorie restriction for sustainable weight loss and long-term health. They suggest that high-quality, unprocessed foods recognized by ancestors are key to feeling full and losing weight effectively. Emphasizing moderation in food quality, the speaker advises against stress-inducing dieting methods.
